expectations.evidence: Code uses role="alertdialog" and aria-modal="true" on the dialog div element

expectations.text: Response implements focus trapping within the modal
expectations.evidence: A useEffect sets up a keydown listener for Tab key, queries focusable elements inside dialogRef, and cycles focus between first and last elements on Tab/Shift+Tab with e.preventDefault()

expectations.text: Response adds Escape key handler to close
expectations.evidence: A separate useEffect adds a keydown listener: if (e.key === 'Escape') { onCancel(); }

expectations.text: Response saves and restores focus to triggering element
expectations.evidence: useEffect saves: previousFocusRef.current = document.activeElement as HTMLElement when open=true, and restores: previousFocusRef.current.focus() when open becomes false

expectations.text: Response uses role=alertdialog for destructive action
expectations.evidence: Code uses role="alertdialog" with explanation: 'role="alertdialog" and aria-modal="true" -- tells screen readers this is a modal dialog that requires user action'
